identity.tagline: Croatia's sunniest island — lavender fields, medieval towns, and a buzzing nightlife.

practical.day_trip_note: Hvar is reachable as a day trip from Split but the island rewards at least one night — the old town comes alive in the evening.

getting_there.ferry_notes: Car ferries run from Split to Stari Grad (the main ferry port, ~2 hours). A faster catamaran runs Split to Hvar town directly (~1 hour, passengers only, no cars or bikes). If driving, use the Stari Grad ferry and drive across the island.

ferry_routes.community_note: Car ferry to Stari Grad. Book car spaces in advance in July and August.
Short car ferry to Sućuraj on the eastern tip of the island. Useful if driving from Dalmatia.

nearby_islands.name: Brač
nearby_islands.description: Home to Zlatni Rat, Croatia's most photographed beach.
nearby_islands.worth_visiting_for: The beach and the town of Bol.

nearby_islands.name: Vis
nearby_islands.description: Remote, quiet, and increasingly popular with travellers seeking the road less taken.
nearby_islands.worth_visiting_for: The Blue Cave and a slower pace than Hvar.

nearby_islands.name: Pakleni Islands
nearby_islands.description: A string of small uninhabited islands just off Hvar town with excellent swimming.
nearby_islands.worth_visiting_for: A half-day boat trip from Hvar town harbour.
